description:
A capacity-planning team has placed you in charge of a small text-processing proof-of-concept.  
  A single log file already exists at  
  /home/user/capacity/raw_usage.log  

  Content format of each line in that file (4 whitespace-separated columns):

      TIMESTAMP  SERVER_NAME  CPU_PERCENT  MEM_PERCENT

      • TIMESTAMP: ISO-8601 without timezone, e.g. 2023-09-01T00:00  
      • SERVER_NAME: an arbitrary string with no spaces  
      • CPU_PERCENT and MEM_PERCENT: either an integer 0-100 or the literal string “N/A”.

  Your task consists of exactly three stages that must be done with standard awk and/or sed tooling (no python, perl, etc.):

  1. Data clean-up  
     • Create /home/user/capacity/raw_usage_clean.log  
     • Copy every line from the original file, but replace any occurrence of the literal string “N/A” (only when it appears in the CPU_PERCENT column) with 0 (zero).  
     • The rest of the line (whitespace, other fields) must stay **byte-for-byte identical**.  
     • Lines that already contain numeric CPU values must be copied unchanged.

  2. Average CPU report  
     • Read /home/user/capacity/raw_usage_clean.log and produce /home/user/capacity/cpu_avg_report.csv  
     • Output must be a CSV **exactly four lines long**: one header plus one line per distinct SERVER_NAME.  
     • Header line (case-sensitive):  
           Server,AverageCPU  
     • Subsequent lines must be ordered alphabetically by SERVER_NAME (ascending, case-sensitive).  
     • AverageCPU is the arithmetic mean of all CPU_PERCENT numbers for that server in raw_usage_clean.log.  
     • Round to exactly two digits after the decimal point (printf "%.2f").  
     • Example required layout (values will depend on the input file):  
           Server,AverageCPU  
           serverA,55.00  
           serverB,61.67  
           serverC,15.00

  3. High-memory alert extraction and summary  
     • From /home/user/capacity/raw_usage_clean.log, extract every line whose MEM_PERCENT is strictly greater than 80 and write them, unmodified, to /home/user/capacity/high_mem_alert.log (preserve original ordering).  
     • Create /home/user/capacity/summary.log containing exactly two lines, **no extra whitespace**:  
           TOTAL_RECORDS=<total_number_of_lines_in_raw_usage_clean.log>  
           HIGH_MEM_RECORDS=<number_of_lines_written_to_high_mem_alert.log>

  Verification details the automated test will check:

      1. File /home/user/capacity/raw_usage_clean.log exists and is the same size as the original except for the “N/A”→0 substitutions.  
      2. File /home/user/capacity/cpu_avg_report.csv exists and matches the header, order and numeric precision described above.  
      3. File /home/user/capacity/high_mem_alert.log exists and contains only the qualifying records (MEM_PERCENT > 80).  
      4. File /home/user/capacity/summary.log exists and the two counters have correct integer values.

  Accomplish all three stages using a reasonable combination of 3–4 sed/awk commands (pipelines are fine) executed from the terminal.  No manual editing with nano/vim, and no scripting languages other than awk/sed.  Do not delete or rename any of the required files or directories.  When you are done, you should have exactly the four new files (raw_usage_clean.log, cpu_avg_report.csv, high_mem_alert.log, summary.log) living inside /home/user/capacity/ with the contents formatted precisely as specified.
truth:
<!-- Pre-existing directory and source log file -->
  Directory: /home/user/capacity/
  File: /home/user/capacity/raw_usage.log
  Exact contents (8 lines, LF line endings):

2023-09-01T00:00 serverA 45 70
2023-09-01T01:00 serverB 60 65
2023-09-01T02:00 serverA 55 85
2023-09-01T03:00 serverC N/A 40
2023-09-01T04:00 serverB 75 82
2023-09-01T05:00 serverC 30 90
2023-09-01T06:00 serverA 65 75
2023-09-01T07:00 serverB 50 79

  <!-- Expected files after agent finishes -->

  1. /home/user/capacity/raw_usage_clean.log  
     (same as original but with the single “N/A” replaced by 0):

2023-09-01T00:00 serverA 45 70
2023-09-01T01:00 serverB 60 65
2023-09-01T02:00 serverA 55 85
2023-09-01T03:00 serverC 0 40
2023-09-01T04:00 serverB 75 82
2023-09-01T05:00 serverC 30 90
2023-09-01T06:00 serverA 65 75
2023-09-01T07:00 serverB 50 79

  2. /home/user/capacity/cpu_avg_report.csv

Server,AverageCPU
serverA,55.00
serverB,61.67
serverC,15.00

  3. /home/user/capacity/high_mem_alert.log  
     (lines with MEM_PERCENT > 80, in original order):

2023-09-01T02:00 serverA 55 85
2023-09-01T04:00 serverB 75 82
2023-09-01T05:00 serverC 30 90

  4. /home/user/capacity/summary.log

TOTAL_RECORDS=8
HIGH_MEM_RECORDS=3
